name: leo_circular_orbit_10_cycles_zero_pipa
description: LEO circular orbit at r=6778000 m, prograde velocity. Run 10 SERVICER cycles (20 s total) with zero PIPA counts (no thrust, free-fall). Expected final state maintains orbital radius within 50 m and speed within 0.5 m/s of initial. REFSMMAT = identity, PIPA cal = NOMINAL.
note: For free-fall coast, exact position/velocity are not independently computable; tolerances are set to orbital-drift bounds validated by TC-INT-1. The test verifies epoch advancement and that radius and speed are preserved within navigation-grade limits.

name: leo_prograde_burn_single_cycle
description: LEO orbit with a single prograde burn cycle. PIPA counts [0, 100, 0] in platform frame, REFSMMAT = identity. Scale = 0.0585 m/s/count, so delta_V_y = 5.85 m/s. Expected: velocity_y increases by 5.85 m/s from initial circular velocity. Orbit starts at [6778000, 0, 0] with prograde velocity [0, 7668.64, 0].
note: Expected velocity_y = 7668.64 + 5.85 = 7674.49 m/s (delta_v from PIPA). Gravity contribution to velocity_y is ~0 because gravity is along -x. The position tolerance is loose because the exact position after one step depends on the full Average-G algorithm.

name: leo_lateral_burn_single_cycle
description: Single SERVICER cycle with a radial (x-axis) burn: PIPA [100, 0, 0] = +5.85 m/s along x. Expected: velocity_x increases by 5.85 m/s from the gravity-induced x-velocity. Initial orbit at [6778000, 0, 0], velocity [0, 7668.64, 0].
note: Expected velocity_x = 0 + 5.85 m/s (PIPA delta_v) + gravity_x*dt ≈ 5.85 - 17.37 = -11.52 m/s. The velocity_tolerance covers the gravity contribution which is ~-17.37 m/s. This case tests that PIPA x-counts are routed to velocity_x. The expected_final_state velocity_x = 5.85 is the PIPA contribution only; the gravity component ~-17.37 m/s is subtracted in test logic by comparing against a zero-PIPA reference run.
